BestPath

Early Detection. Better Outcomes.

Identifying overdue screenings and connecting patients with the care they need — powered by clinical guidelines and vector database technology.

Capital Reasoning Systems  ·  Victoria, BC
Peter Salmon  ·  Nicholas Miller
capitalreasoning.com  ·  April 2026

The Problem
6.5M

Canadians have no family doctor.

Clinicians managing panels of 1,500+ patients cannot manually track who is overdue for which screening against hundreds of clinical guidelines. When preventive care is missed, conditions escalate — a diabetic without an A1C check in two years, a 55-year-old never screened for colorectal cancer. These are preventable emergencies.

4,620
Died on BC waitlists last year
352K
Waiting for a family doctor in BC
32 wks
Specialist referral to treatment

Every missed screening matters. The cost of delay is too high.

Technology Inflection

Why this wasn’t possible before.

BEFORE

  • Clinical guidelines published as PDFs
  • Patient records locked in EMR systems
  • Matching them: manual chart review

Months

to screen 1,500 patients

NOW

  • LLMs parse and understand guideline documents
  • Vector databases index them by meaning
  • RAG pipelines match patients to relevant guidelines

Minutes

to screen 1,500 patients

The convergence of large language models, vector databases, and retrieval-augmented generation makes this a deployable product — not a research project — for the first time.

The Solution

Two inputs. One output.

Input

Health Guidelines

4,000+ provincial and federal clinical guidelines — BC clinical practice guidelines, CTFPHC, BC Cancer, Diabetes Canada, Hypertension Canada, and more — parsed, chunked, and embedded into a vector database.

Input

Patient Data

Individual patient demographics, active conditions, medications, vitals, and encounter history — imported from EMR systems. The system builds a complete risk profile for every patient in the panel.

▼
Output

Targeted, Evidence-Cited Outreach

The system analyzes patient profiles against condition-specific screening recommendations and intervals. It determines what’s overdue, prioritizes by clinical urgency, and — with a single action — initiates outreach via email and SMS, informing patients what needs to be done and where to access care.

Deterministic comparison. Every recommendation traced to a specific guideline passage. No AI in the clinical scoring loop.

The Product

Care Review Dashboard

375 patients analyzed against clinical guidelines in minutes. Triage distribution, screening timelines, and age-group breakdowns at a glance.

Urgent Follow-up On Track
The Workflow

From assessment to patient outreach.

A single action initiates targeted outreach via email and SMS.

Patient assessment with evidence citations

One-click outreach via email and SMS

Every recommendation cites its source guideline — section and page number. The clinician reviews, approves, and sends. The patient receives clear instructions: what needs to be done and where to access care.

Trust

AI suggests. Math decides.

Clinical Guidelines

Define what’s needed.
4,000+ documents cited to specific section and page.

Patient Data

Show what’s been done.
EMR records with timestamped encounters and observations.

Deterministic Math

Confirm what’s overdue.
Auditable scoring. No opaque model. Fully verifiable.

AI reads the guidelines and generates natural language explanations.
It never determines the triage category — the math does.

Regulatory Positioning

Designed for Health Canada SaMD exclusion — provides information for clinician decision-making, not autonomous clinical decisions. Clinicians approve every recommendation. Fully auditable chain from recommendation to source guideline.

Market

Who needs this.

Entry Market

Primary Care Clinics

50,000+ clinics across Canada. Every family practice managing patient panels too large to manually screen against clinical guidelines.

Scale Market

Health Authorities

Provincial systems managing population-level preventive care — with real budgets and mandates for digital health adoption.

Premium Market

Private & Senior Care

Concierge clinics offering deep preventive medicine. Retirement homes ensuring residents get timely screenings — a care quality and liability imperative.

200 clinics × $8K/year = $1.6M ARR — before health authorities, premium tiers, or international expansion

Expansion:   Now: BC guidelines  →  Next: National (CTFPHC)  →  Then: International — UK, Australia, NZ. The engine is guideline-agnostic.

Business Model

On-premise. Privacy-first.

Patient data never leaves the clinic’s network.

Most AI health tools are cloud-based — patient data goes to external servers. BestPath deploys on the clinic’s own infrastructure, eliminating privacy and compliance barriers.

Installation & Integration
One-time
Deploy on clinic hardware, integrate with existing EMR, configure guideline corpus.
Annual Platform License
Recurring
Software updates, security patches, and technical support.
Guideline Corpus Updates
Recurring
Clinical guidelines change constantly. We maintain the curated knowledge base.

The curated guideline knowledge base is the product. Clinics subscribe to the intelligence — not the compute.

Delta Hotels Victoria

Capital Reasoning Systems — Victoria, BC. Building AI-powered information retrieval products since 2024.

We’re AI and information retrieval specialists who found a healthcare problem our exact skill set can solve. Clinical domain expertise is the first gap we’re addressing with funding.

The Ask
$50,000

From hackathon project to first paying customer.

Clinical Expertise

~40% · $20K

Healthcare researcher to validate and expand the guideline corpus. Domain credibility and clinical accuracy — the most critical hire.

Infrastructure

~35% · $17.5K

On-premise demo hardware and production deployment. Harden the platform for real clinical use.

Go-to-Market

~25% · $12.5K

Clinic pilot with 2–3 family practices. Outreach to health authority innovation teams.

Target: first paying clinic within 6 months — validates the product, generates revenue, positions for a seed round.

This is bridge capital — we’re not building a company on $50K. We’re proving it works in the real world.
